question: What is the actual all-in cost of selling on each major Chinese e-commerce platform, and how has it changed?

findings.claim: Chinese e-commerce platform all-in merchant costs (commissions + advertising) are approximately 4-5% of GMV on Alibaba and Pinduoduo, and up to ~10% on Douyin
findings.data: Reconstructions of Chinese platforms' accounts suggest commissions plus advertising amount to roughly 4-5% of GMV for Alibaba and Pinduoduo, close to 10% for short-video marketplaces like Douyin. Sponsored listings account for 1-3% of GMV for Indian marketplaces, 4-5% for Alibaba and Pinduoduo, close to 10% for short-video platforms.

findings.claim: Alibaba introduced 0.6% software service fee on all Taobao and Tmall transactions starting September 1, 2024, replacing fixed annual fees for Tmall merchants
findings.data: Alibaba began charging a basic software service fee of 0.6% on confirmed transactions for vendors on both Tmall and Taobao platforms starting September 1, 2024. Previously, Alibaba charged a fixed annual fee (30,000-60,000 yuan) to Tmall merchants, which was waived. Taobao C2C stores were previously exempt from transaction-based fees. Small merchants earning less than 120,000 yuan annually receive full refunds of the service fee.

findings.claim: Alibaba's Quanzhantui (full-site promotion) penetration rate near 30% in 2025, expected to reach 45-50% in FY2026
findings.data: The penetration rate of full-site promotion (Quanzhantui) is now close to 30%, and it is expected to reach 45%-50% in fiscal year 2026. The software service fee will continue to drive up the take rate.

findings.claim: PDD Holdings charges merchants a technical service fee of approximately 0.6-1% of GMV since 2020, with a 10 billion yuan fee waiver pledge for 'high-quality' merchants
findings.data: PDD started charging merchants a technical service fee of about 0.6%-1% of GMV since 2020. PDD chairman Chen Lei said it would waive 10 billion yuan in transaction fees for 'high-quality' merchants over the next year, willing to accept short-term profitability declines. After policy changes, Pinduoduo began returning some service fees to merchants in promotional campaigns.

findings.claim: Traffic promotion costs account for over 50% of a typical Tmall merchant's total operating costs
findings.data: Traffic promotion costs account for over 50% of a typical merchant's total operating costs on Tmall. Platform service fees consume another 5-7%. After-sales and logistics add 5%. This means roughly 60-70% of operating budget goes to costs beyond platform fees and product costs.

findings.claim: Tmall commission rates range from 0.5-10% depending on category, typically 2-5%
findings.data: Some categories of merchants on Tmall need to pay a software service fee according to a certain percentage of their sales. This fee is typically either charged at 2% and 5%. However, this can vary between 0.5% to 10% for some edge cases. JD.com charges 2-5% commission for the majority of categories.

findings.claim: Douyin invested 5.5 billion yuan in merchant subsidies in Q1 2025 alone, with total merchant cost savings of 32 billion yuan for full year 2025
findings.data: Following over 3 billion yuan in cost reductions for merchants in Jan-Feb, Douyin increased support in March, reducing commissions and costs by over 2 billion yuan in that single month. Total cost savings for Q1 2025: 5.5 billion yuan. By end of August 2025, Douyin subsidized merchants by more than 19 billion yuan. For full year 2025, cumulative merchant cost savings exceeded 32 billion yuan.

findings.claim: SAMR drafted 28-article guidelines to regulate e-commerce platform fees, requiring transparency and banning unfair practices
findings.data: SAMR released 'Compliance Guide for Online Trading Platform Fees' covering commissions, membership fees, technical service charges, information fees, and marketing expenses. Key provisions: platforms must publish commission fees prominently; solicit public opinion for 7 days before pricing changes; keep historical pricing records for 3 years; ban on duplicate charging, passing costs to merchants, unreasonable security deposits. Comment period ended June 3, 2025.
